Tanya and Brad had a positive discussion and received answers to many of their questions about becoming FedEx Linehaul contractors and the value of the runs. They were advised that there is no "perfect" opportunity in linehaul; the important thing is getting started and learning along the way. Questions and challenges will continue to evolve, and operating the business will require ongoing adjustments based on available opportunities and personal preferences. Their child/family considerations will play a significant role in their decision-making. They have cash available and are preparing to make an offer, but there is no urgency, and they were encouraged to discuss it further on Monday. It was explained that there isn't much room for negotiation in the business. The current trucks are not considered a major factor, since it is usually better to purchase newer trucks with warranties that fit their needs rather than inherit existing equipment. Another conversation is planned for the following day, and if terms can be worked out, Tanya and Brad are expected to move forward with an offer. Rick had a productive conversation with prospective buyers Tanya and Brad, answering their questions about entering the FedEx Linehaul business. He emphasized that there is no perfect opportunity and that success comes from getting started and adapting over time. Since they have the cash and are highly interested, they are expected to move forward with an offer if the terms can be worked out. Rick also noted that the existing trucks are not a major factor, as buyers are often better off purchasing trucks that fit their needs.;Buyer Notes – Brad Matthews Morgan spoke with Tanya (Brad Matthews' wife) regarding the Noki opportunity and the valuation materials. Morgan worked with Wren to identify and correct the issue. Action Item: Going forward, ensure all unassigned run revenue is included in weekly revenue calculations and that P&Ls are thoroughly reviewed before distribution. Wren can assist with additional reviews when needed. Morgan also walked Tanya through: The LIM meeting process Common questions asked during LIM meetings P&L structure and financial breakdowns Peak season considerations Truck/equipment-related discussions Buyer appears to be primarily interested in SBA financing, though cash purchases may be possible depending on the opportunity. Exact available cash position was not discussed or confirmed.

Because of the amount of time and resources invested throughout the process, the company normally collects the consulting fee once the LIM meeting is completed. In Swetha's case, they proposed collecting only half of the fee upfront due to the specifics of the transaction, and she was understanding and comfortable with the arrangement. As a next step, Tucker should send Swetha an unsigned LOI along with a clear explanation of the deal terms.
